Squaring these, we get \( (8k \pm 1)^2 = 64k^2 \pm 16k + 1 = 8(8k^2 \pm 2k) + 1 \), so \( p^2 \equiv 1 \pmod{8} \). 3. **Modulo 5**: Any prime \( p > 5 \) is of the form \( 5k \pm 1 \) or \( 5k \pm 2 \). Since \( x \equiv 1 \pmod{8} \) and \( x \equiv 1 \pmod{3} \), we have \( x \equiv 1 \pmod{24} \) (since 8 and 3 are coprime). Now we need to solve:

This can be written as \( x = 24k + 1 \) and \( 24k + 1 \equiv 4 \pmod{5} \), which simplifies to \( 24k \equiv 3 \pmod{5} \) or \( -k \equiv 3 \pmod{5} \) or \( k \equiv 2 \pmod{5} \).
